references.name: NHS App web integration overview
references.borrowed: Embedded webviews should hide duplicated supplier chrome and behave like part of the NHS App.
references.rejected: No NHS-App-only workflow fork was introduced.

references.name: NHS App JavaScript API v2
references.borrowed: Native bridge behavior is centralized; route content does not call JS API methods directly.
references.rejected: No pharmacy route depends on browser-only downloads, external navigation, or unverified bridge state.

references.name: NHS service manual error message
references.borrowed: Errors and blocked states explain the next step instead of acting like eligibility denial copy.
references.rejected: No inline error is shown while the user is merely browsing provider rows.

references.name: NHS accessibility design guidance
references.borrowed: Single main landmark, clear headings, contrast-aware restrained palette, and visible focused controls.
references.rejected: No nested app shell or duplicated NHS header was added.

references.name: NHS accessibility content guidance
references.borrowed: Labels and recovery instructions stay direct and connected to the control or state.
references.rejected: No hidden-only action labels for core pharmacy decisions.

references.name: NHS choosing a pharmacy help
references.borrowed: Chosen pharmacy remains a patient-facing concept and can be changed only when the route permits it.
references.rejected: Prescription nomination copy was not reused as clinical referral proof.

references.name: NHS find pharmacy services
references.borrowed: Location and service availability are useful secondary cues.
references.rejected: The embedded journey is not a generic public pharmacy locator.

references.name: WAI-ARIA APG disclosure
references.borrowed: Warned-choice detail uses a disclosure button with expanded state and controlled content.
references.rejected: Disclosure is not used to hide mandatory recovery guidance.

references.name: WAI-ARIA APG landmarks
references.borrowed: The embedded route keeps landmark count small and meaningful.
references.rejected: No decorative regions were promoted to landmarks.

references.name: Playwright ARIA snapshots
references.borrowed: Playwright tests capture ARIA structure for chooser, action reserve, and instructions.
references.rejected: ARIA snapshots do not replace interaction and visual checks.

references.name: Playwright emulation
references.borrowed: Mobile webview tests use narrow viewport, locale, user agent, and reduced motion.
references.rejected: Device emulation is not treated as native device API proof.

references.name: Playwright trace viewer
references.borrowed: Longer visual journeys keep a trace artifact for triage.
references.rejected: Trace output is not the only verification evidence.

references.name: Linear UI redesign
references.borrowed: Reduced visual noise and stronger hierarchy informed the list-first surface.
references.rejected: No dark command-center aesthetic was copied into NHS patient content.

references.name: Vercel nested layouts
references.borrowed: Persistent route shell continuity shaped the masthead, summary, and action reserve.
references.rejected: No framework-specific routing dependency was introduced.

references.name: Carbon data table usage
references.borrowed: Dense facts use compact rows and restrained metadata grouping.
references.rejected: Provider choice is not rendered as a spreadsheet-like table.
